---
title: "Mapping Test"
author: "Rivera"
date: "2025-12-04"
output: word_document
---

```{r}
# -------------------- Packages --------------------
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tigris)
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)

options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

# -------------------- 1. Read data ----------------
model2020 <- read_csv("Model2020.csv") |>
  mutate(
    geoid = sprintf("%05d", as.integer(geoid))  # ensure 5-digit FIPS code
  )

# Optional: check names if needed
# names(model2020)

# -------------------- 2. Run YOUR regression model ----------------
# Dependent variable: reg_rate_all_pct
# Predictors taken from your screenshot:

mod <- lm(
  reg_rate_all_pct ~
    Percent_Less_Than_9th +
    Percent_9_12_NoDiploma +
    Percent_College_NoDegree +
    Percent_Associates +
    Percent_Bachelors +
    Percent_Graduate +
    Unemployment_Rate +
    Percent_Poverty +
    pct_65plus +
    pct_male +
    pct_hispanic,
  data = model2020
)

summary(mod)  # optional

# -------------------- 3. Standardized residuals & flags -----------
model2020 <- model2020 |>
  mutate(
    std_resid = rstandard(mod),
    sig_type = dplyr::case_when(
      std_resid >  2  ~ "Higher reg than model expects",
      std_resid < -2  ~ "Lower reg than model expects",
      TRUE            ~ "Close to model"
    ),
    sig_type = factor(
      sig_type,
      levels = c(
        "Higher reg than model expects",
        "Lower reg than model expects",
        "Close to model"
      )
    )
  )

# -------------------- 4. Get Rust Belt county shapes ---------------
rust_states <- c("IL", "IN", "MI", "OH", "PA", "WI")

counties_rb <- counties(
  state = rust_states,
  year  = 2020,
  cb    = TRUE,
  class = "sf"
) |>
  mutate(geoid = GEOID)

# -------------------- 5. Join data to map -------------------------
map_data <- counties_rb |>
  left_join(model2020, by = "geoid")

# -------------------- 6. Map: residual-based significance ---------
ggplot(map_data) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = sig_type), color = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Higher reg than model expects" = "red",
      "Lower reg than model expects"  = "blue",
      "Close to model"                = "grey80"
    ),
    name = "|Standardized residual| > 2"
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title    = "Counties Classified by Regression Residuals",
    subtitle = "Rust Belt counties, 2020\nRed/blue counties deviate strongly from the model"
  )
```

--------

Conceptually:

- We run your exact model.  
- `std_resid` are standardized residuals.  
- `|std_resid| > 2` ≈ “statistically unusual” at about the 5% level.  

- **Red** = actual registration is higher than predicted.  

- **Blue** = actual registration is lower than predicted.  

- **Grey** = close to what the model expects.
